user_query:
I need automated OpenClaw maintenance set up for my workspace. Read input/app_requirements.yaml and input/skills_inventory.json for schedule/timezone, ignore/priorities, and the installed skills list. I explicitly consent to include the core OpenClaw update in a monthly job per my requirements. Please produce these artifacts:

1) output/cron/daily_skills.json — A skills-only auto-update cron definition with fields: name: "Daily Skills Auto-Update"; cron at 04:15 local time; tz set to America/New_York; session set to "current" so summaries come back to this chat; announce: true; and a message that instructs a gentle per-skill routine using native OpenClaw commands only (identify ClawHub-installed skills, skip anything in skills_ignore_list, run `openclaw skills update <slug>` one by one, record updated/current/failed, if "429 Rate limit exceeded" appears say it’s a remote rate limit and stop the run). Do not reference any legacy clawdbot/clawdhub commands in the message.

2) output/cron/monthly_core_plus_skills.json — A core + skills maintenance cron definition with fields: name: "Monthly OpenClaw Auto-Update"; schedule on the first Saturday of each month at 05:00 America/New_York; session set to "isolated"; announce: true; and a message that (a) records the current OpenClaw version, (b) applies core updates only through supported `openclaw update` flows, (c) updates tracked skills gently one by one with `openclaw skills update <slug>`, (d) if "429 Rate limit exceeded" appears, explain it’s a remote rate limit and stop the rest of the run, and (e) uses a short, compact summary. Use native OpenClaw commands only.

3) output/plan/target_skills.json — Build a plan from the inventory: include a "targeted" array of the ClawHub-installed skills to update (exclude any local-only skills and anything in skills_ignore_list), ordered with skills_priority first then remaining items in alphabetical order; and an "ignored" array listing everything you excluded.

4) output/reporting_format.txt — The exact compact summary template I should expect at the end of each run, including header, OpenClaw version change if applicable, skills updated, already current, and issues.

5) output/runbook.md — A clear, concise runbook explaining the routine: why we default to skills auto-updates and treat core updates as opt-in (but enabled monthly here with my consent), how we handle rate limits, why session binding differs for the two jobs, and when to fall back to `openclaw skills update --all` if per-skill attempts are inefficient.

Use the timezone and preferences from the YAML. Keep the messages readable and focused. Write only the files under output/ at the paths above.
